title: rhus aromatica subsp. serotina
intro: Rhus aromatica subsp. serotina, also known as the Fragrant Sumac or Mountain Sumac, is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of the eastern United States. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Rhus aromatica subsp. serotina include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
